sanskrit: कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसंमूढचेताः। यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्चितं ब्रूहि तन्मे शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम्॥
transliteration: kārpaṇya-doṣopahata-svabhāvaḥ pṛcchāmi tvāṁ dharma-saṁmūḍha-cetāḥ | yac chreyaḥ syān niścitaṁ brūhi tan me śiṣyas te ’haṁ śādhi māṁ tvāṁ prapannam ||
literal_meaning: Afflicted by the defect of miserliness, my nature is overcome; I ask You, my mind bewildered about dharma. Tell me decisively what is the highest good for me; I am Your disciple, instruct me who has surrendered to You.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna moves from intellectual argument to raw vulnerability: he names his paralysis as a ‘miserly weakness,’ admits cognitive overload, and shifts role from friend to student, requesting authoritative guidance rather than more debate.
psychological_state: Acute decision-paralysis with ego-dystonic shame; cognitive deconstruction of prior values; transition from autonomous hero to supplicating learner.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career physician facing a choice between withdrawing life-support for a loved one and prolonging suffering—feels intellectually equipped yet emotionally frozen, finally turning to a trusted mentor with ‘Tell me what is right; I give up deciding alone.’
anchor_text: Admit your confusion and humbly seek clear guidance.
